prompt.content:
I am interested in learning more about the small set of borrowers with the highest critical exposure. Save the data from 'risk_integrated_data.csv' as an Excel file and use this to perform the rest of the analysis. Using the Excel file, first identify the top borrower for each industry with the single highest individual expected loss amount record (a.k.a. critical exposure). Do not aggregate or average the expected loss amounts per borrower.

Next, create a new worksheet called 'Final' where for each of the aforementioned borrowers, report their change in liquid cash (new - old) from the earliest available month to the latest available month, relative to each borrower, rounded to 3 decimal places. Liquid cash, in this scenario, is calculated as the current balance - real world asset amount. Finally, in the same worksheet 'Final', make a recommendation of which one borrower should should be removed from future loans based on the largest decrease in liquid cash (most negative change).

For each top critical exposure borrower by industry, only report the change in liquid cash and recommendation of company to remove from future loans. Create any helper worksheets as needed and make sure to use Excel formulas (not hard-coded values) so results automatically update if any of the underlying data changes.

rubrics.name: Identifies Company_13127_Manufacturing as the top critical exposure borrower... (ver_16c75ec58c7a431fadb5b709e45495ea)

messages.content: Identifies Company_13127_Manufacturing as the top critical exposure borrower for the Manufacturing industry.
